TextENGNew: ...Lunch break must be almost over by now.
Muttering that inwardly, I sat up from the nurse's office bed and glanced at the wall clock.
I felt a twinge of disappointment at how little time had actually passed. Until now, I'd sometimes panicked at how quickly time flew by, but never once had I felt weary of its sluggish crawl.
A faint scent of disinfectant hung in the air, lending the white room a vaguely clinical chill.
Some people say they hate this kind of atmosphere, but having been a research subject since early childhood, I'd grown accustomed to it. If anything, I found it calming.
My back and the nape of my neck ached slightly. ...I must have dozed off at an awkward angle.
"Just... twenty more days..."
changeReason: Changes numbers: old has {'20'}, new has set().

TextENGNew: Staring at the calendar on the wall, I murmured the number of days remaining until the "day of destiny."
I had been sent back one month this time. For some reason, it was longer than the last.
Whether that would prove a blessing or a curse... I still couldn't say. There was no way to know.
And yet, pathetically enough, I had already squandered ten whole days in idle, listless inactivity.
changeReason: Changes numbers: old has {'10'}, new has set().

TextENGNew: I have to act. I know that.
I have to think. I know that too.
If things continue as they are, that *witch* will get her hands on us again, and we'll be led to the worst possible end.
But... I know all that, and yet...
I clenched the comforter. The white, freshly laundered sheet cover crumpled instantly, drinking in the sweat from my palms.
But that was all it could do. It offered no answers.
And the one who would always be there at times like this, pestering me with answers whether I wanted them or not -- was gone.
When I realized that, the first things I felt were an overwhelming *terror* and an indescribable *loneliness*.
It was strange.
I was surrounded by such lively, boisterous friends.
Whenever I walked through Hinamizawa or Okinomiya, every villager would press their hands together in reverence and shower me with kindness.
It's true I have no blood relatives... but there had never been any reason for me to feel alone.
And yet... despite having lived through hundreds, perhaps thousands of years, this was the very first time I found myself gripped by this sensation of utter solitude.
And as that aching pain throbbed deep within my chest, I was cutting myself off from everyone -- as though I were running away. ...That was who I had become.
The name slipped from my lips, barely a whisper.
Maybe she would answer. Maybe she'd suddenly appear beside me, saying something ridiculous like "I was just trying to surprise you, Rika, so I played a little trick" --
Or maybe she really had intended to do that, but some kind of trouble made her miss her chance, and now she was too afraid of being scolded to show herself.
...Each time I caught myself clinging to such feeble hopes, the self-loathing only deepened. ...A true vicious cycle.
*Knock, knock...*
"Excuuuse me.@k@vS14/06/210600129. ...Oh, Rika-chama, you're awake."
The door opened before I could respond, and in walked Shion. In one hand she carried a stacked lunch box -- my meal, most likely.
"How are you feeling?@k@vS14/06/210600131. ...Oh, you look a little better than before."
"...Mii. My head and body still feel heavy."
changeReason: Changes Rika's catchphrase rendering from 'Meep' to 'Mii' (みぃ).

TextENGNew: "That's odd timing for summer fatigue, though...@k@vS14/06/210600133. I'll leave your lunch right here, Rika-chama.@k@vS14/06/210600134. I brought apples and fruit salad -- things that are easy on the stomach."
"...Thank you. Nipah~."
I smiled, and Shion returned it with one of her own.
...How strange. Was Shion always this gentle?
Setting aside my suspicions, Shion placed the lunch box on the nearby desk and came to my side, pressing her hand against my forehead.
...Her touch was cool, but soft and soothing.
"You don't seem to have a fever.@k@vS14/06/210600136. I was thinking of dropping by for dinner again tonight... would that be all right?"
"Please don't worry about me.@k@vS14/05/210500055. If you don't come, I'll be stuck with Satoko's... creative cooking. That's just a tiny bit dreadful."
"Ahahahaha!@k@vS14/06/210600138. Satoko would be furious if she heard that, you know?"
"Of course, it's our little secret.@k@vS14/05/210500057. If you tell, Oyashiro-sama's curse will come for you, Shii."
"Ooh~, how terrifying.@k@vS14/06/210600140. When the shrine maiden Rika-chama says it, it doesn't exactly sound like a joke."
Shion made an exaggerated show of surprise, but her eyes were still smiling. She was just playing along with my joke -- but right now, even that felt faintly unpleasant.
...No, that wasn't quite it. Something slightly different.

It felt unpleasant because it was as though she could see right through me. I had invoked the name of "Oyashiro-sama" -- the one who was no longer at my side -- and it felt like she was pointing that out...
...What am I even thinking?
Of course Shion hadn't noticed anything. Even regarding the villagers' worship of me as the "reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama," she probably saw me as little more than the village mascot.
Shion had been away from Hinamizawa for a long time. Because of that, she had always held relatively realistic -- even mildly skeptical -- views about the curse... with one notable exception.
"Well, I should get going.@k@vS14/06/210600142. What about you, Rika-chama?@k@vS14/06/210600143. If you want to leave early, I've called a car -- we could ride together."
"Mii... I'm feeling a bit better after that nap.@k@vS14/05/210500059. Once I eat, I'll do my best to make it to afternoon class."
changeReason: Changes Rika's catchphrase rendering from 'Meep' to 'Mii' (みぃ).

TextENGNew: "I see.@k@vS14/06/210600145. Just don't push yourself too hard, okay?@k@vS14/06/210600146. Well then --"
"Oh -- Shii."
"Yes?"
Shion was heading for the door with a smile when I called out to stop her. When she turned back, I felt a pang of regret... but I steeled myself and decided to ask.
"Shii...@k@vS14/05/210500062. Since when have you and Satoko been so close?"
Just as I expected, Shion blinked in bewilderment, her eyes going round. Of course she'd think it was an odd question... My regret kept mounting, but it was too late to take it back.
"...You're surprisingly close.@k@vS14/05/210500064. You must be well aware of the bad blood between the Sonozaki and Houjou families, Shii."
"Ahahahaha!@k@vS14/06/210600150. If that's the standard, the same applies to Sis, doesn't it?"

changeReason: Changes Rika's catchphrase rendering from 'Meep' to 'Mii' (みぃ).

TextENGNew: Come to think of it, she had a point. What was I even asking?
Perhaps sensing my flustered state, Shion leaned in with a sly grin.
"Hmm~, is it really so strange for Satoko and me to get along?@k@vS14/06/210600152. ...Oh~? Could it be that you're jealous, Rika-chama?@k@vS14/06/210600153. Afraid I'm stealing her away~?"
"...Mii~. Satoko is my adorable playmate.@k@vS14/05/210500067. I won't hand her over to you, Shii. Nipah~."
changeReason: Changes Rika's catchphrase rendering from 'Meep' to 'Mii' (みぃ).

TextENGNew: "Aahahaha!@k@vS14/06/210600155. A love triangle over Satoko!@k@vS14/06/210600156. I can practically see the lilies blooming behind us~."
...I didn't say anything that extreme.
At any rate, I was relieved the conversation had veered off track.
"Besides... I was entrusted with it, you know.@k@vS14/06/210600158. Looking after Satoko."
"...By Satoshi?"
Shion's eyes widened slightly, as if caught off guard.

I thought I'd made a mistake bringing up his name, but Shion quickly shrugged and broke into a playful smile. Her expression seemed to say "go ahead and laugh if you want" -- yet there was an unmistakable resolve burning in her eyes.
"...Well, sure.@k@vS14/06/210600161. Right after what happened with Satoshi-kun, things were... complicated."

"I started to wonder if maybe Satoko was partly responsible for Satoshi-kun's disappearance...@k@vS14/06/210600163. Looking back now, it's laughable -- but there was definitely a time I held that kind of misplaced resentment."
I knew. And in one of the timelines I had lived through, that resentment had metastasized into murderous hatred -- directed at the Sonozaki family, and at Satoko...
I didn't believe Satoshi-kun would come back.
I didn't believe Satoshi-kun was alive.
Because I didn't think he was alive, I assumed he was dead.
Yes -- I believed in his death.
I killed him. Inside my own heart.
No one else did that. ...I did.
But Satoko believed, right up to the very end.
And... in her final moments, that faith was rewarded.
Satoko was no longer here.
But I was still here.
Satoshi-kun... had been waiting for Satoko, and for me.
But to see Satoshi-kun again, we had to prove we could keep our promises.
Satoko's promise... was to become strong.
Satoko kept that promise to the bitter end.
...And Satoshi-kun took her into his arms.
My promise... was to look after Satoko in Satoshi-kun's place.
I never kept that promise. Not once, from the very beginning.
And so... Satoshi-kun... would never welcome me.
At this point, even if I stroked Satoko's head and asked "Does it hurt?"... it wouldn't mean a thing.
That was when I sensed something behind me.
The figure standing there, expressionless, was neither Satoshi-kun nor that eerie presence that sometimes frightened me.
I didn't know who it was.
...It just stood there in silence, wordlessly accusing me of my failures.
...And then... I finally understood. It was... me.
I had always been standing behind myself, watching as I broke that promise with my own hands.
Satoshi-kun had entrusted me with Satoko's care, and I had been watching myself discard that promise all along.
...That other me wept, and... abandoned even this version of myself...
Wait... Satoshi-kun abandoned me, and now even I've abandoned myself... What happens to me then...?
Please don't leave me behind...
If my own self has cast me aside... what becomes of this body...?
"Shii...?"
"Huh? Oh, sorry about that."
"Anyway, a little while back, after that baseball game...@k@vS14/06/210600166. I had a chance to talk with Satoko, thanks to Kei-chan."
"And I asked her something a bit mean-spirited.@k@vS14/06/210600168. 'When Satoshi-kun comes back, what's the first thing you'll do for him?'@k@vS14/06/210600169. ...What do you think she said?"
"I wonder...?"
"'I'll apologize.' ...She said it with a completely serious face."
"She said Satoshi-kun left because she kept clinging to him and acting spoiled.@k@vS14/06/210600172. So first she'd apologize -- and then show him how strong she'd become."
"...That girl understood her own sin all along."
Shion smiled and shook her head as she spoke. There wasn't the faintest trace of the fierce hostility toward Satoko I had witnessed in other timelines.

"Satoko is trying to become stronger.@k@vS14/06/210600175. She sees it as her promise and her atonement.@k@vS14/06/210600176. So that when Satoshi-kun returns, she'll never be a burden to him again..."
"She believes.@k@vS14/06/210600178. That as long as she keeps that promise, he'll come home someday."
"Shii..."
"...Satoko and I are in the same position.@k@vS14/06/210600180. So I'll keep my promise too, and keep looking after her... That's how I've come to feel."
"Believing is hard, but nothing begins without belief... I feel like someone said that once.@k@vS14/06/210600182. ...If you can't answer someone's trust, you have no right to believe in miracles."
"...No right, you say..."
Those casual words carried a terrible weight.
Perhaps sensing the solemnity in my expression, Shion gave an embarrassed laugh and scratched her head.
"...J-just kidding! That's not like me at all, is it? Who do I think I am, Sis?"

"...Well, lately Satoko's reactions and mannerisms have gotten so small-animal-like that teasing her is irresistible. She's actually become pretty adorable, you know?@k@vS14/06/210600185. ...Teehee~."
T-teehee...?? Was Shion always the type to say that...?
But still...
"...I'm sure it will come true.@k@vS14/05/210500075. I'll be praying that your wish and Satoko's wish both come to pass."
"Ahahaha, is that an official declaration from Oyashiro-sama's shrine maiden?"
"...Oyashiro-sama has nothing to do with it.@k@vS14/05/210500077. But if it ever becomes necessary, I'll put in a word with Oyashiro-sama myself."
"I appreciate that.@k@vS14/06/210600188. ...But I'm not relying on divine intervention. I intend to do my part on my own.@k@vS14/06/210600189. Well then --"
With that, Shion raised a hand in a casual wave and left the nurse's office.
Such an offhanded exit. ...But I had only recently come to understand: the closer Shion was to revealing her true feelings, the more she feigned indifference.
...If you can't answer someone's trust, you have no right to believe in miracles, huh...
For a while, I sat in bed gazing absently at the scenery outside, turning over that conversation with Shion in my mind.
From far away came the crisp, pleasant ringing of a bell.
Afternoon classes seemed to be starting.
"All right!@k@vS14/03/210300066. In accordance with club bylaws, I wish to put a motion before all members!"
The moment after-school arrived, Mion gathered us around our desks as usual and made her proclamation in a booming voice.
"Our club has become renowned not only throughout Hinamizawa, but all across Okinomiya as well!"
"Renowned...? Don't you mean notorious?"
"K-Keiichi-kun..."
"Therefore!@k@vS14/03/210300069. As our sphere of activity expands, and with it the variety of opponents we must face, a bolstering of our forces is in order!@k@vS14/03/210300070. To that end --"
"You're just ignoring me?!"
"In short, she wants to recruit new members."
"Renaaaa!@k@vS14/03/210300072. Don't steal the best line!!"
...Wait, she delivered that whole propaganda speech just for that one punchline? Let me be blunt: what a waste of time.
"But yeah, getting more members actually does sound fun!@k@vS14/01/210100046. Whether it's ball games or anything else, more people means more variety and more excitement!"
"Rena has nooo~ objections!"
"Ohohohoho!@k@vS14/04/210400098. No matter who we recruit, I can't imagine any of them being a match for me!"
"...Satoko and I both agree."
In response to Mion's proposal, all three -- including Rika-chan, who had returned after lunch -- voiced their approval. And of course, I was all for it too.
"That's all well and good, but... who exactly are we adding?"
No matter how much we wanted to recruit, it was pointless without willing candidates. And anyone joining our club would need both the skill and the willpower to handle what we put them through.
"Tomita-kun?@k@vS14/02/210200043. ...Or maybe Okamura-kun?"
"If it's those two, I'd love to set the entry requirements impossibly high~!"
Still sore about being betrayed during the earlier exchange with Shion, Satoko crossed her arms and fixed Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun with an icy glare.
The moment their eyes met hers, they flinched and clasped their hands together... Indeed, even if those two wanted to join, the screening would be brutal... especially Satoko's portion.
"Tsk, tsk, tsk... You really can't figure it out?@k@vS14/03/210300074. They're right in front of you."
"R-right in front of us...?"
We all looked around the classroom at Mion's prompting. ...But since the entire class was still there, it was impossible to tell who she meant...
Everyone?
Isn't that a bit odd?
Even after school, most students should have been packing up to leave by now. And yet every single one of them was still here, watching us with expectant eyes...
"No way...?"
"That's right! Exactly!!@k@vS14/03/210300076. The candidates for our club are -- the entire class!!!"
""Please let us join!!""
On Mion's cue, every student who had surrounded us without our noticing let out a thunderous shout in unison -- part greeting, part audition.
"A-amazing...!"
"I-I had no idea everyone was so aware of our club activities...!"
Both Rena and Satoko stood dumbfounded at the sheer scale. I was no different.
"...With this many people, we could even field an American football team."
No, that's way too physically demanding for these kids...
"But what an incredible turnout!@k@vS14/01/210100052. Yeah, absolutely! I officially welcome --"
Just as I was about to accept,
"Howeeever!@k@vS14/03/210300078. Our club has an ironclad rule!!@k@vS14/03/210300079. Any applicant must pass a rigorous entrance exam!!!"
"Huuuh?!"
"M-Mii-chan?!"
We stared in disbelief. Since when did we have a rule like that?
...No, she definitely just made that up on the spot.
"Now!@k@vS14/03/210300081. Do you all have the courage and grit to overcome this trial?!"
Every student answered immediately, all smiles, nodding vigorously. Satisfied, Mion gave a grand nod of her own and made her declaration.
"Then the entrance exam will be held tomorrow!@k@vS14/03/210300083. And the event will be... kick the can!!"
